channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats running for New Jersey governor navigate the delicate politics of immigration
item.description: New Jersey’s governor’s race this year, one of just two along with Virginia, is emerging as a proving ground for Democrats struggling to find a message that can win over voters.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US prepares to deport about 300 alleged gang members to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration will pay El Salvador $6 million to imprison for one year about 300 alleged members of the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang, in one of the first instances of the Central American country taking migrants from the United States.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration from implementing Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal judge expanded his ruling temporarily blocking the Trump administration from invoking the Alien Enemies Act of 1798 on Saturday preventing the removal of Venezuelan undocumented immigrants presumed to be members of the Tren de Aragua gang. The order issued by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs a bill funding the government for 6 months, avoiding a shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed into law legislation funding the government through the end of September, ending the threat of a partial government shutdown and capping off a struggle in Congress that deeply divided Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fetterman claps back at Ocasio-Cortez on government funding vote
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who announced days ago that he would vote for a House-passed funding bill to avoid a government shutdown, said he doesn’t care about firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s (D-N.Y.) harsh criticism of Democratic senators who will vote to advance the controversial bill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump allies mount campaign to get DOGE codified by Congress
item.description: A bill by Trump's House GOP allies would codify a reporting structure between DOGE and the presidential Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Infighting to Irrelevance
item.description: The current Democratic infighting and political malpractice over forcing or not forcing a government shutdown exposes a leaderless party that believes in opposing Donald Trump and issues that 80% of Americans agree with.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Republican legislation seeks to ban Chinese nationals from studying in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of House Republicans on Friday put forward legislation seeking to prevent Chinese students from studying in American schools, as some U.S. lawmakers are targeting China over national security concerns. Rep. Riley Moore, R-W.Va.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man escorted out of NC Republican's town hall
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards (R-N.C.) faced a wave of backlash from constituents while hosting a Thursday town hall in Asheville, which he held against the advice of party leaders. One attendee, who identified himself as a veteran, was removed from the event. “He has nothing to say but lies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: AOC, Sanders tell supporters to mask up for West Coast town halls
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., are taking the "Fighting Oligarchy" rallies out West next week – and advising supporters to mask up to participate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mace sued for defamation over allegations in stunning floor speech
item.description: A man Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of being part of a group of “predators” in a stunning House floor speech is suing her for defamation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Musk meets with head of National Security Agency to ensure it is aligned with Trump, spy agency says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk met this week with the leader of the National Security Agency, suggesting the nation's spy services won't escape the billionaire's scrutiny as he looks to slash spending and personnel across the federal government. Musk met Wednesday with Gen.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE lease cancellations: These government offices could be closed in Minnesota
item.description: The General Services Administration, working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, has notified landlords that federal agencies will be terminating hundreds of leases for offices and buildings in the coming months — including a dozen in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hibbing manufacturer responds to Trump‘s steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Canada is the biggest supplier of steel and aluminum for the U.S. The metals are used for products like nuts and bolts, sheet metal, machining and beer cans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Raúl Grijalva dies at 77
item.description: Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D-Ariz.), a more than two-decade veteran of the House who rose to high levels of power in the Democratic Caucus, died on Thursday. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: How the Trump administration’s policies are affecting farmers
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about how President Trump’s tariffs and funding cuts could affect Minnesota farmers.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Fired federal worker from Minnesota appeals Trump administration job cuts
item.description: A fired federal worker from Minnesota is part of a class action appeal of the mass terminations being carried out by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens retaliatory 200% tariff on European wine after EU proposes American whiskey tax
item.description: The European Commission is calling on President Donald Trump to revoke tariffs now that he's threatened a 200 percent tariff on European wine, Champagne and spirits if Europe goes forward with a planned tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Red light cameras coming to Las Vegas? Lawmaker wants exception that would allow them
item.description: Traffic cameras to catch drivers in the act of running red lights would be allowed under a bill introduced Tuesday at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. inflation cooled last month, though trade war threatens to lift prices
item.description: U.S. inflation slowed last month for the first time since September even as additional tariffs on steel and aluminum kicked in Wednesday that could send prices higher.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
